Two 15-300 um Si surface-barrier detectors on
rotatable table
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases
SD: Major corrections in all Subents.
Subents 020-026 added.
14N targets of 150-350 mu-g/cm2 thickness were
prepared by evaporating melamine onto carbon backing.
No information about source of
uncertainty.

SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated. ERR-T -> DATA-ERR; E-EXC -> E-LVL.
Enriched 25Mg targets of 40 to 200 ug/cm2 thickness
were produced by evaporation on carbon films of 20 to
40 ug/cm2 thickness. The starting material used was a
mixture of 25MgO enriched to 98.4% and tantalum
powder.
The absolute determination of the
25Mg(d,alpha) cross-sections was carried out with
targets of the natural Mg isotope composition, the
thickness of which was known.
No information about source of
uncertainty.

Silicon targets having a thickness of 60 to 200 ug/cm2
were produced by evaporation of a mixture
of Si and SiO onto thin carbon films.
Due to the known chemical composition,
precisely weighed targets from the walls of a quartz
bubble (SiO2) were used to determine the absolute
cross- sections.
No information about source of
uncertainty.

SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code. BIB
updated. ERR-T->DATA-ERR; E-EXC-MIN/MAX->E-LVL-MIN/MAX.
Self-supporting foils of natural isotope composition
of 120-200 mu-g/cm2 thickness.
The error in the measurements is a maximum
of +- 10% for the higher effective cross-sections
> 0.3 mb/sr. It is mainly determined by the statistical
error. At the lower effective cross-sections
< 0.3 mb/sr, where the substrate is noticeably
disturbed, the error is about 30 mu-b/sr.
Error in target thicknesses and of
inhomogeneity of the targets
By compiler: To present DATA-ERR in one column,
compiler merged two types of error presentation.

SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated. ANG-ERR -> ANG-ERR-D; DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG;
DATA-ERR1/2 -> DATA-ERR; E-EXC-MIN/MAX -> E-LVL.
The accuracy of data presentation has been revised.
Enriched 25Mg targets of 40 to 200 ug/cm2 thickness
were produced by evaporation on carbon films of 20 to
40 ug/cm2 thickness. The starting material used was a
mixture of 25MgO enriched to 98.4% and tantalum
powder.
The absolute determination of the 25Mg(d,alpha) cross-
sections was carried out with targets of the natural
Mg isotope composition, the thickness of which was
known.
The error in the measurements is a maximum
of +- 10% for the higher effective cross-sections
> 50 mu-b/sr. It is mainly determined by the
statistical error. At the lower effective cross-
sections < 50 mu-b/sr, where the substrate is
noticeably disturbed, the error is about 5 mu-b/sr.
Error in target thicknesses and of
inhomogeneity of the targets
By compiler: To present DATA-ERR in one column,
compiler merged two types of error presentation.
Fig.4 from Nucl.Phys.,76(1966)79
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated. ANG-ERR -> ANG-ERR-D; DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG;
DATA-ERR1/2 -> DATA-ERR; E-EXC -> E-LVL. Data units
were corrected (mb/sr -> mu-b/sr, see fig.4).
The accuracy of data presentation has been revised.
Enriched 25Mg targets of 40 to 200 ug/cm2 thickness
were produced by evaporation on carbon films of 20 to
40 ug/cm2 thickness. The starting material used was a
mixture of 25MgO enriched to 98.4% and tantalum
powder.
The absolute determination of the 25Mg(d,alpha) cross-
sections was carried out with targets of the natural
Mg isotope composition, the thickness of which was
known.
The error in the measurements is a
maximum of +- 10% for the higher effective cross-
sections > 50 mu-b/sr. It is mainly determined by the
statistical error. (At the lower effective cross-
sections < 50 mu-b/sr, where the substrate is
noticeably disturbed, the error is about 5 mu-b/sr.
Error in target thicknesses and of
inhomogeneity of the targets
All cross-sections greater 50 mu-b/sr in this Subent.

SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated. ANG-ERR -> ANG-ERR-D; DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG;
DATA-ERR1/2 -> DATA-ERR; E-EXC-MIN/MAX -> E-LVL. Data
units were corrected (mb/sr -> mu-b/sr, see fig.4).
The accuracy of data presentation has been revised.
Silicon targets having a thickness of 60 to 200 ug/cm2
were produced by vapor deposition of a mixture
of Si and SiO onto thin carbon films.
Due to the known chemical composition,
precisely weighed targets from the walls of a quartz
bubble (SiO2) were used to determine the absolute
cross- sections.
The error in the measurements is a maximum
of +- 10% for the higher effective cross-sections
> 0.3 mb/sr. It is mainly determined by the statistical
error. At the lower effective cross-sections
< 0.3 mb/sr, where the substrate is noticeably
disturbed, the error is about 30 mu-b/sr.
Error in target thicknesses and of
inhomogeneity of the targets
